$comment: Reference case for coulomb_friction (Ft_clamped = min(Ft, mu*Fn)). The min() has two branches: when Ft < mu*Fn the output equals Ft and carries NO information about mu. Ft is pinned high (50 N) so the min() always selects the mu*Fn branch — the output is then exactly mu*Fn, linear in mu and well-conditioned. Canonical mu=0.4 differs from the library default 0.5 so the fitter must actually recover it (not start on the answer).
notes: 12-point sweep over normal force. With Ft=50 N and mu*Fn ≤ 0.4*10 = 4 N, the clamp never triggers, so output = mu*Fn — a clean linear fit. If a future change made the min() select the wrong branch, this case would break immediately. Friction coefficient mu=0.4 is a typical wood-on-wood value.
